metadata.description: Scale-local calibration for percentage point effect sizes
metadata.note: Calibration is scale-local. Deployment follows validation, not ambition.

workflow: 1. Run LLMMC protocol → get pp_llm, EU_llm (in pp units)
5. Optional: map to θ-scale using pp/50 (for integration)

scientific_justification.why_scale_local: pp effects have different base rates and ceiling effects than standardized effect sizes
scientific_justification.why_separate_from_d: Forcing pp onto d-scale loses information; calibrate in native units first
scientific_justification.key_insight: After adding A9-A12 (high-pp defaults), leverage artifacts disappeared
scientific_justification.reviewer_statement: Percentage-point effects are calibrated on Tier-1/2 anchors spanning the full support (4–87 pp).

relation_to_d_calibration.note: This calibration is INDEPENDENT of calibration_d_v1.json
relation_to_d_calibration.integration: Both can be used; choose based on effect metric in source study
relation_to_d_calibration.mapping: If needed, map calibrated pp to θ via: θ = min(1, pp/50)
